handoff.salientSummary: The Tauri stage now emits and consumes Electron-compatible window lifecycle state. Rust snapshots focused/minimized/visible state from WebviewWindow, emits electron:window:lifecycle-changed on state changes, and the renderer shows reactive bounds plus lifecycle diagnostics while the Live2D stage remains visible.
handoff.whatWasImplemented: Added lifecycle contracts to @proj-airi/tauri-eventa, mapped lifecycle invokes to electron_window_get_lifecycle_state, implemented real Rust lifecycle snapshots and a setup-time polling emitter, introduced @proj-airi/tauri-vueuse with Electron-compatible Eventa context helpers and useElectronWindowBounds, and added a stage-tauri Pinia lifecycle store consumed by App.vue.
handoff.whatWasLeftUndone: The lifecycle emitter uses polling because Tauri's Linux window event stream does not expose every minimize/restore transition directly. Later transparent-window and multi-window features can revisit this if a lower-level Wayland signal source is added.

commandsRun.observation: 1 test file passed; 8 tests passed, including lifecycle invoke mapping.
vue-tsc --noEmit passed after rerunning sequentially behind tauri-vueuse build. An earlier parallel run raced the dependency's dist clean step and failed to resolve @proj-airi/tauri-vueuse.
vue-tsc and Vite production build passed with existing DuckDB/browser externalization, import.meta, large chunk, and plugin timing warnings.
Rust build passed with known placeholder dead-code warnings.
Init script passed and cargo check completed with known placeholder dead-code warnings.
User confirmed the Tauri stage was running well and the Live2D character works.

interactiveChecks.description: Main Tauri window lifecycle/bounds UI with Live2D stage
interactiveChecks.result: User confirmed the running Tauri stage and Live2D character were working after the lifecycle feature changes.

tests.coverage: Adapter test covers the lifecycle Eventa invoke mapping. Runtime/user verification covers the Tauri stage staying usable with the Live2D renderer while the lifecycle bridge is active.

discoveredIssues.description: Running stage-tauri typecheck in parallel with tauri-vueuse build can race against tsdown's dist cleanup because stage-tauri resolves @proj-airi/tauri-vueuse through package exports.
Rust build still reports known placeholder dead-code warnings in auth, server_channel, and window command structs.
MCP Serena/jcodemunch tools required by AGENTS.md were unavailable, so native tooling fallback was used and recorded.

deviations.step: AGENTS.md MCP startup and delegated code-change loop
deviations.whatIDidInstead: Used native rg/sed/git/apply_patch/pnpm/cargo tooling, plus user runtime confirmation.
deviations.why: The MCP tools named by AGENTS.md were not exposed in the current tool list.
